# Let's read the file content
path = "/mnt/data/file-BqeWZo8eHnjdg2tVKckK6p-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:500]

import re, json, textwrap

text = open("/mnt/data/file-BqeWZo8eHnjdg2tVKckK6p-text_to_analyse.txt","r",encoding="utf-8").read()

# find substrings between standard ASCII double quotes
quote_spans = [(m.start()+1, m.end()-1, text[m.start()+1:m.end()-1]) for m in re.finditer(r'"[^"]*"', text)]
quote_spans, len(quote_spans)

# Find substrings within quranic ornaments
orn_spans = [(m.start()+1, m.end()-1, text[m.start()+1:m.end()-1]) for m in re.finditer(r'﴿[^﴿﴾]*﴾', text)]
orn_spans, len(orn_spans)

import re

text = open("/mnt/data/file-BqeWZo8eHnjdg2tVKckK6p-text_to_analyse.txt","r",encoding="utf-8").read()

# find all bracket spans
bracket_spans = [(m.start(), m.end(), text[m.start():m.end()]) for m in re.finditer(r'\[[^\]]+\]', text)]
len(bracket_spans)

# filter to those that look like sura: aya citations (contain colon and Arabic letters)
def is_quran_citation(bracket_text):
    # must contain colon ':' and at least one Arabic letter
    return ':' in bracket_text and re.search(r'[\u0600-\u06FF]', bracket_text) is not None

quran_brackets = [b for b in bracket_spans if is_quran_citation(b[2])]
len(quran_brackets), quran_brackets[:10]

delims = set('\n.?!()[]«»"…،؛:')  # define delimiters
# We'll also include the Arabic parenthesis marks if present - but not necessary
def find_phrase_before(idx_start):
    # Move left from idx_start-1 ignoring spaces at end
    i = idx_start - 1
    # Trim trailing spaces before bracket
    while i >= 0 and text[i].isspace():
        i -= 1
    # Now find the previous delimiter
    start = 0
    for j in range(i, -1, -1):
        if text[j] in delims:
            start = j + 1
            break
    else:
        start = 0
    # Strip leading spaces
    while start < len(text) and text[start].isspace():
        start += 1
    # Also strip leading decorative characters like "—" or opening parentheses? We already stopped at delimiter, so ok.
    return start, i+1, text[start:i+1]

phrases = []
for s,e,bt in quran_brackets:
    st, en, substr = find_phrase_before(s)
    phrases.append((st,en,substr,bt))
phrases

# Find "قرأ:" and "تلا:" patterns
import re
results = []
for m in re.finditer(r'قرأ:\s*', text):
    start = m.end()
    # find end at next period or newline
    end = start
    while end < len(text) and text[end] not in '.\n':
        end += 1
    # trim whitespace at end
    while end > start and text[end-1].isspace():
        end -= 1
    results.append((start, end, text[start:end]))
results

results2 = []
for m in re.finditer(r'تلا:\s*', text):
    start = m.end()
    end = start
    while end < len(text) and text[end] not in '.\n':
        end += 1
    while end > start and text[end-1].isspace():
        end -= 1
    results2.append((start, end, text[start:end]))
results2

# Find 'قرأ' exact substring and show context
i = text.find('قرأ:')
i, text[i-30:i+50]

import re
m = re.search(r'قرأ:\s*', text)
m, text[4447:4452].encode('unicode_escape')

# Find "قال تعالى:" positions and show following string to ensure captured
for m in re.finditer(r'قال تعالى:\s*', text):
    idx = m.end()
    print(m.start(), text[idx:idx+60])

# Gather all spans
spans = []

# from double quotes (excluding the Hasan al-Basri quote one)
double_quote_spans = [(182, 219, 'إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ لَأَوَّاهٌ حَلِيمٌ'),
  (262, 307, 'إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ لَحَلِيمٌ أَوَّاهٌ مُنِيبٌ'),
  (394, 426, 'فَبَشَّرْنَاهُ بِغُلَامٍ حَلِيمٍ'),
  (514, 660, 'قَالَ يَا قَوْمِ لَيْسَ بِي سَفَاهَةٌ وَلَكِنِّي رَسُولٌ مِنْ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ * أُبَلِّغُكُمْ رِسَالَاتِ رَبِّي وَأَنَا لَكُمْ نَاصِحٌ أَمِينٌ'),
  (1107, 1139, 'فَبَشَّرْنَاهُ بِغُلَامٍ حَلِيمٍ'),
  (1280, 1350, 'إِنَّا لَنَرَاكَ فِي سَفَاهَةٍ وَإِنَّا لَنَظُنُّكَ مِنَ الْكَاذِبِينَ')]

spans.extend(double_quote_spans)

# from ornaments
orn_spans = [(1850, 1887, 'إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ لَأَوَّاهٌ حَلِيمٌ'),
  (1910, 1955, 'إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ لَحَلِيمٌ أَوَّاهٌ مُنِيبٌ'),
  (1991, 2023, 'فَبَشَّرْنَاهُ بِغُلَامٍ حَلِيمٍ'),
  (2059, 2097, 'إِنَّكَ لَأَنْتَ الْحَلِيمُ الرَّشِيدُ')]
spans.extend(orn_spans)

# from bracket extractions prior function 'phrases'
phrases = [(944, 965, 'إن إبراهيم لأواه حليم'),
 (981, 1009, 'وإن إبراهيم لحليم أوّاه منيب'),
 (2304, 2341, 'إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ لَأَوَّاهٌ حَلِيمٌ'),
 (2358, 2404, 'وإِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ لَحَلِيمٌ أَوَّاهٌ مُنِيبٌ'),
 (2606, 2638, 'فَبَشَّرْنَاهُ بِغُلَامٍ حَلِيمٍ'),
 (2695, 2902, 'فَلَمَّا بَلَغَ مَعَهُ السَّعْيَ قَالَ يَا بُنَيَّ إِنِّي أَرَى فِي الْمَنَامِ أَنِّي أَذْبَحَكَ فَانْظُرْ مَاذَا تَرَى قَالَ يَا أَبَتِ افْعَلْ مَا تُؤْمَرْ سَتَجِدُنِي إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ مِنَ الصَّابِريِنَ'),
 (2985, 3034, 'سَتَجِدُنِي إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ مِنَ الصَّابِرِينَ'),
 (3204, 3423, 'إِنَّا لَنَرَاكَ فِي سَفَاهَةٍ وَإِنَّا لَنَظُنُّكَ مِنَ الْكَاذِبِينَ * قَالَ يَا قَوْمِ لَيْسَ بِي سَفَاهَةٌ وَلَكِنِّي رَسُولٌ مِنْ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ * أُبَلِّغُكُمْ رِسَالَاتِ رَبِّي وَأَنَا لَكُمْ نَاصِحٌ أَمِينٌ')]
spans.extend(phrases)

# from 'قرأ:'
spans.append((4452, 4497, 'إِنِّي أَرَى فِي الْمَنَامِ أَنِّي أَذْبَحُكَ'))

# from 'تلا:'
spans.append((4871, 4937, 'إني أرى في المنام أني أذبحك فانظر ماذا ترى قال يا أبت افعل ما تؤمر'))

# Deduplicate by start,end
unique = []
seen = set()
for s in spans:
    key = (s[0], s[1])
    if key not in seen:
        seen.add(key)
        unique.append(s)

len(unique), unique

# Verify substrings match text at positions
all_ok = True
for s,e,t in unique:
    if text[s:e] != t:
        print("Mismatch at", s, e, repr(text[s:e]), "vs", repr(t))
        all_ok = False
all_ok
